title: Fix persistent admin payments table not loading and payment submission failures

requirements.text: Audit and fix the backend `getAllPayments` query function in `backend/main.mo` to guarantee that all records written by `submitPayment` are persisted in stable storage and correctly returned by `getAllPayments`. Verify: (1) `submitPayment` appends to the same stable variable that `getAllPayments` reads from; (2) stable storage is not re-initialised on canister upgrade (use `preupgrade`/`postupgrade` hooks if needed); (3) no records are silently dropped or shadowed by a transient in-memory buffer. Add a defensive check so `getAllPayments` returns an empty array (not an error) when no records exist.
acceptanceCriteria: `submitPayment` writes each new record to the stable variable used by `getAllPayments`.
`getAllPayments` returns all previously submitted records after a canister upgrade.
Calling `getAllPayments` on an empty store returns an empty array without throwing.
No in-memory buffer exists that would cause records to be lost on upgrade.

requirements.text: Completely rewrite the actor initialisation and query-gating logic in `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` and `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts`. The rewrite must: (1) expose an `actorReady` boolean that is `false` until the `AuthClient` has fully resolved and the correct identity (anonymous or authenticated) has been applied to the actor instance; (2) ensure the actor reference is stable and non-null before any query or mutation is allowed to fire; (3) for `useGetAllPayments`, set `enabled: !!actor && actorReady && isAuthenticated` and configure `retry: 3` with exponential back-off (`retryDelay: attempt => Math.min(1000 * 2 ** attempt, 10000)`); (4) after a successful Internet Identity login, call `queryClient.invalidateQueries(['getAllPayments'])` so the table refetches automatically without a manual page refresh; (5) for `useSubmitPayment`, gate the mutation on `!!actor && actorReady` and add `retry: 2` with exponential back-off so brief startup delays do not surface as immediate failures.
acceptanceCriteria: `actorReady` is `false` during `AuthClient` initialisation and becomes `true` only after the identity is applied.
`useGetAllPayments` does not fire until `actorReady && isAuthenticated` are both true.
After Internet Identity login completes, `getAllPayments` is invalidated and refetched automatically.
`useSubmitPayment` does not fire while the actor is not ready; the submit button shows a disabled/spinner state instead.
Both hooks retry at least 2–3 times with exponential back-off before surfacing an error state.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/AdminPage.tsx` to: (1) show a loading spinner whenever `useGetAllPayments` is in a loading or retrying state (do not render the error UI while retries are still in-flight); (2) display the 'Failed to load payments' error only after all retries are exhausted and `actorReady && isAuthenticated` are confirmed true; (3) render every row returned by the query — Full Name, Email, Address, masked Card Number (last 4 digits), Expiry Date, Amount — so records submitted via the payment form appear in the table immediately after login without a manual refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: A spinner is shown while the query is loading or retrying.
The error message is rendered only after all React Query retries are exhausted.
All payment rows returned by the backend are rendered in the table with correct column values.
Card numbers are masked to show only the last 4 digits.
The table populates automatically after login without requiring a page refresh.

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/components/PaymentForm.tsx` to ensure the submit button is disabled with a loading spinner whenever the actor is not yet ready (`!actorReady`), preventing the 'Payment service not ready' error from being shown to the user. The mutation should only be triggered once the actor is confirmed ready. If a submission fails after all retries, surface the actual rejection reason from the backend in the error message rather than a generic fallback string.
acceptanceCriteria: The submit button is disabled and shows a spinner while `actorReady` is false.
The 'Payment service not ready' error is never shown; users see a spinner instead.
After all retries are exhausted, the error message reflects the actual backend rejection reason when available.
Successful submissions still show the confirmation message and reset the form.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in backend/main.mo (single-actor architecture).
Preserve all existing payment form fields, validation logic, and light amber/gold theme.
nonGoals: Changing the visual theme or color palette.
Modifying the Internet Identity authentication flow itself.
Adding new admin features beyond displaying the payments table.
